ps140: O venerable and holy martyred hierarch,* shining sacredly with the light of the priesthood,* you emitted the unfading radiance of martyrdom.* And now you have passed over to the never-waning effulgence,* wherein the choirs of the martyrs rejoice,* and where the ranks of angels join chorus.* With them be ever mindful of us, O blessed and God-pleasing one.

O Cyril, martyr and athlete, manifest as a servant of God you ministered to Christ;* and, sacrificed as a pure immolation,* you were offered up upon the altar of God, O divinely blessed one,* and have received a precious inheritance,* ever abiding amid the splendours of the saints,* holy of calling and deed.

O all-praised martyrs of Christ,* you passed without harm through the furnace of torments* with the dew of divine grace,* and have been vouchsafed to dwell by still waters,* having received the prize of victory.* Wherefore, rejoicing today, we celebrate with faith your sacred memory,* O holy ones, glorifying Christ.
theotokion: You do we entreat, O all-pure one,* our intercessor:* Let not your servants perish utterly* who are troubled amid griefs;* but haste to rescue us* from this present wrath and sorrow,* O divinely joyous, pure and all-holy one;* for you are a rampart for us* and help invincible.
stavrotheotokion: Seeing You, O Jesus,* nailed to the Cross* and accepting sufferings voluntarily,* the Virgin, Your Mother, cried out, O Master:* "Woe is me, O my sweet Child!* How is it that You endure wounding unjustly,* O Physician Who heales the afflictions of men* and deliveres all from corruption* in Your lovingkindness?"
